content:
- Executed task 02 (pgBackRest managed archive/recovery + config takeover).
- Implemented Rust-native Postgres `archive_command`/`restore_command` ownership:
  - Added `pgtuskmaster wal` helper subcommand invoked by Postgres.
  - Added `src/backup/archive_command.rs` to materialize `PGDATA/pgtm.pgbackrest.archive.json` and render pgBackRest `archive-push`/`archive-get` commands deterministically.
  - Updated `src/postgres_managed.rs` to materialize helper config + inject `archive_mode=on`, `archive_command`, `restore_command`; also write `PGDATA/pgtm.postgresql.conf`.
- Updated operator docs (`docs/src/operator/configuration.md`, `docs/src/operator/observability.md`, `docs/src/operator/recovery-bootstrap-runbook.md`) to reflect owned wiring.
- Added/extended tests to cover missing helper config errors and takeover/materialize wiring file creation.
- Verified all gates are green (rerun after final changes): `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`.
- Marked task file done/passing, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed and pushed.

- Next: Continue story task 03+ (replica/standby wiring, `standby.signal` ownership, `primary_conninfo` in task 05), and consider adding deeper recovery failure integration tests if we want explicit WAL-mismatch scenarios.

- Read progress log and scanned `.ralph/current_tasks.md`.
- Confirmed there are no failing bug tasks; all bug tasks show `<passes>true</passes>` / `<passing>true</passing>`.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>`; selected next most logical task as the next step in the already-in-progress pgBackRest managed backup/recovery story chain.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/04-task-rust-generic-argv-passthrough-binary-for-postgres-archive-restore-logging.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and loaded task 04: `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/04-task-rust-generic-argv-passthrough-binary-for-postgres-archive-restore-logging.md`.
- Attempted to spawn 20+ `explore_spark` subagents for parallel research, but all failed due to a usage-limit lockout until ~04:12.
- Proceeded with local repo inspection (rg/sed) and wrote a detailed execution plan into the task file.
- Plan focuses on: absolute-path-only binary validation (no PATH), bounded stdout/stderr capture with truncation flags in `pgtuskmaster wal`, and a new `/events/wal` API endpoint so the helper can emit structured events into central pgtuskmaster logs.

- Next: Return after the plan is verified (TO BE VERIFIED phase). Perform deep skeptical review and adjust at least one part of the plan, then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Investigated current code paths for binary/path resolution to confirm PATH dependence.
- Confirmed `Command::new` is fed `PathBuf`s from config for pg tools, `pgbackrest`, and wal helper, with no absolute enforcement in most paths.
- Found explicit absolute validation helper exists but is only applied to some logging/data paths, not process binaries.
- Confirmed `archive_command` currently `absolutize_path`s relative values against current dir instead of rejecting them.
- Confirmed `self_exe::get_or_fallback()` silently falls back to `pgtuskmaster`, preserving PATH lookup in helper mode.
- No code edits in this turn; preparing mitigation proposal only.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and the task file `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/04-task-rust-generic-argv-passthrough-binary-for-postgres-archive-restore-logging.md`.
- Task file already had a draft plan ending with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Attempted 20+ `explore_spark` subagents immediately, but all errored due to model usage limit until ~04:12 CET.
- Waited until `2026-03-05 04:12 CET`, then re-ran 20+ explore_spark subagents for deep skeptical verification.
- Incorporated subagent findings into the plan and changed the marker to `NOW EXECUTE`:
  - Prefer `reqwest` blocking client (enable `blocking` feature) for helper -> node event POST; no ad-hoc tokio runtime.
  - Replace `/bin/sh -c <archive_command>` execution tests with direct `pgtuskmaster wal ...` subprocess tests; keep only string-rendering assertions for postgresql.conf.
  - Add loopback-only peer enforcement for `POST /events/wal`.
  - Add defense-in-depth absolute-path checks at spawn boundaries; call out `self_exe::get_or_fallback` PATH fallback as a bug to remove.

- Next: DO NOT explore; execute the `NOW EXECUTE` plan step-by-step, ticking checkboxes, then run `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint` until 100% green; update docs; then mark `<passes>true</passes>` and follow the task-switch/commit/push procedure.

- Executed task 04 NOW EXECUTE plan: enforced absolute-path-only binaries (no PATH fallback), added loopback-only POST /events/wal ingest endpoint, and implemented WAL helper passthrough runner with bounded stdout/stderr capture + best-effort event emission.
- Extended PGDATA helper config (pgtm.pgbackrest.archive.json) to include api_local_addr + optional token; changed file mode to 0600; removed absolutize_path behavior and fail-closed on non-absolute paths.
- Added deterministic tests: config validation rejects relative binaries, /events/wal endpoint ingestion + concurrency, wal passthrough integration tests invoking pgtuskmaster wal, and managed postgres config string placeholder quoting check.
- Updated docs: observability/configuration/runbook + node-api to document backup.wal_passthrough event stream and /events/wal endpoint; documented absolute-path requirements and api.listen_addr port constraint.
- Ran and passed: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint. Installed/attested real binaries via tools/install-*.sh.
- Next: update task file status/passes, run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit+push with evidence-backed message.
